title: Mineral Ion Reference Chart
description: Ion contributions per gram per gallon for common brewing mineral additions.
units: ppm (mg/L) per gram per US gallon

minerals.name: Gypsum
minerals.notes: Primary source of calcium and sulfate. Accentuates hop character and dryness.

minerals.name: Calcium Chloride
minerals.notes: Adds calcium without sulfate. Accentuates malt character and roundness.

minerals.name: Epsom Salt
minerals.notes: Source of magnesium and sulfate. Use sparingly — excess magnesium is a laxative.

minerals.name: Baking Soda
minerals.notes: Raises alkalinity and pH. Use for dark beers brewed with soft water.

minerals.name: Table Salt (non-iodized)
minerals.notes: Adds sodium and chloride. Use iodine-free salt only — iodine inhibits yeast.

minerals.name: Chalk (Calcium Carbonate)
minerals.notes: Poorly soluble. Best added directly to mash. Used for dark beers to raise alkalinity.

minerals.name: Pickling Lime (Calcium Hydroxide)
minerals.notes: Strong base. Use very carefully. Raises pH significantly. Useful for reducing carbonate hardness when combined with CO₂.
